jokes.text: Just pretend we're blind. What's the matter with that?
jokes.setup: Larry wants to avoid meeting Richard in person
jokes.punchline: His solution is to pretend they're both blind, as if that makes phone conversation equivalent to face-to-face
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - solving a social problem with an absurd equivalency. Very character-specific reasoning.

jokes.text: You don't have to back up. I don't think it's contagious. Is it contagious?
jokes.setup: Larry tells someone they don't need to back away from his dermatitis
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals his own uncertainty by asking if it's contagious
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction joke - reassures others while being unsure himself.

jokes.text: Can't Rosa take you? She drives right by there.
jokes.setup: Cheryl doesn't want to drive separately to dinner
jokes.punchline: Larry suggesting their housekeeper chauffeur his wife instead of him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry selfishness - using household staff to avoid minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Even with the whole affirmative action thing?
jokes.setup: Larry meets Richard's black dermatologist
jokes.punchline: Makes an inappropriate affirmative action joke about the doctor's qualifications
jokes.explanation: The central cringe moment - Larry's attempt to be 'affable' backfires spectacularly. High impact due to social transgression.

jokes.text: I see it in a historical sense, but not in a nice-day sense.
jokes.setup: Dr. Grambs explaining why the comment was offensive
jokes.punchline: Larry's bizarre distinction between historical and 'nice-day' racism
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Larry logic attempting to rationalize his way out of offense with meaningless distinctions

jokes.text: Holy shit! What hit you?
jokes.setup: Someone sees Larry after the affirmative action incident
jokes.punchline: Reacts as if Larry looks physically beaten up from embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Visual callback to Larry's embarrassment - treating social mortification as physical damage.

jokes.text: Holy shit! What hit you?
jokes.setup: Larry's visible embarrassment after the affirmative action incident
jokes.punchline: Richard's shock at Larry's apparently disheveled state
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing the physical toll of Larry's social mortification

jokes.text: I tend to say stupid things to black people sometimes. That's, like, gonna be in the time capsule.
jokes.setup: Larry reflects on his mistake
jokes.punchline: Self-aware confession with absurd historical importance analogy
jokes.explanation: Meta-awareness of his own character flaw plus absurd time capsule reference. Quotable and self-aware.

jokes.text: I tend to say stupid things to black people sometimes.
jokes.setup: Larry reflecting on his affirmative action blunder
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact admission of a pattern of racial foot-in-mouth disease
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cringey Larry self-awareness delivered with zero filter or shame

jokes.text: I was so discombobulated because... Oh, my God, I completely humiliated...
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he forgot the prescription
jokes.punchline: His self-absorbed focus on his own humiliation while his wife suffers
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry selfishness - making his wife's medical emergency about his social embarrassment

jokes.text: Why would you even make a joke about that? Why even joke about it?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his affirmative action comment
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's repeated incredulity at Larry's poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction establishing the audience's perspective on Larry's stupidity

jokes.text: Men do this, right? I've seen men do it. Haven't we seen this? I've seen men do it in movies.
jokes.setup: Larry preparing to bribe the maitre d'
jokes.punchline: Their nervous consultation about bribery technique based on movie knowledge
jokes.explanation: Funny nervousness about attempting something they've only seen in movies

jokes.text: Things are done, right, aren't things done?
jokes.setup: Larry hints at wanting to bribe the maitre d'
jokes.punchline: Uses vague euphemisms instead of being direct about bribery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry-speak - being indirect about something obvious. The vague phrasing is funnier than direct bribery.

jokes.text: That's 'cause it's criminal. Women are attracted to criminals.
jokes.setup: Cheryl says she's attracted to Larry after the successful bribery
jokes.punchline: Larry attributes it to women being attracted to criminals
jokes.explanation: Pop psychology observation about criminal attraction applied to minor restaurant bribery.

jokes.text: How can a handicapped person use a non-motorized wheelchair?
jokes.setup: Larry observes someone in a manual wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Questions the logic of manual wheelchairs with characteristic over-analysis
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry over-thinking of everyday situations. Character-specific but not his strongest observation.

jokes.text: Going to another restaurant to use the bathroom
jokes.setup: Larry needs to use the bathroom but will pass Ted Harbert
jokes.punchline: Decides to leave the restaurant entirely to avoid small talk
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd solution to social anxiety. Pure Larry logic - extreme measures to avoid minor discomfort.

jokes.text: There's a restaurant two doors down. I'm gonna go in there. I'm gonna go to the bathroom in there.
jokes.setup: Larry wanting to avoid Ted Harbert
jokes.punchline: His elaborate plan to leave the restaurant just to avoid saying hello
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Larry's social avoidance - the lengths he'll go to dodge small talk

jokes.text: Are you out of your mind? No, I don't wanna say hello. I don't like those hellos.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's disbelief at Larry's restaurant-hopping bathroom plan
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact explanation of his hatred for social greetings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry categorization of social interactions - 'those hellos' as a specific type of torture

jokes.text: I watched your show, Seinfeld, for seven, eight years, and not one black person.
jokes.setup: Denise confronts Larry about not hiring her
jokes.punchline: Calls out the actual lack of diversity on Seinfeld
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the actual show's casting. Brave self-criticism that's both funny and pointed.

jokes.text: Is it a black thing with Larry David?
jokes.setup: Denise's analysis of Seinfeld's diversity problems
jokes.punchline: Her direct accusation of racial prejudice
jokes.explanation: Escalates the race theme while putting Larry in maximum discomfort

jokes.text: I saw Sour Grapes, and the title was very apropos.
jokes.setup: Denise delivers her final insult to Larry
jokes.punchline: Uses the title of Larry's actual movie as a perfect insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of real movie title as character assassination. Meta and cutting.

jokes.text: The prescription/money mix-up revelation
jokes.setup: They discover Larry gave the prescription to the maitre d' instead of money
jokes.punchline: The bribery backfired because he bribed with medical paperwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the bribery plot. Larry's incompetence creates a new crisis.

jokes.text: Norman, is somebody calling? Who's calling? We're in the middle of dinner, Norman!
jokes.setup: Larry imitates his doctor's wife preventing weekend calls
jokes.punchline: Creates an elaborate domestic scenario about the doctor's home life
jokes.explanation: Larry's imaginative rant about why doctors won't take weekend calls. Good character voice work.

jokes.text: "Norman, is somebody calling? Who's calling? We're in the middle of dinner, Norman! This better be life-threatening, or you're not gonna leave this house!"
jokes.setup: Larry ranting about weekend doctor policies
jokes.punchline: His elaborate impression of the doctor's domineering wife
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation with Larry creating entire domestic scenario

jokes.text: "Norman... unless they were burned in a fire... I don't want you getting up from your chair. Do you understand, Norman?"
jokes.setup: Larry's impression of the doctor's controlling wife
jokes.punchline: The wife's extreme standards for medical emergencies
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the doctor impression with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: You'll find it in a trash can, right through those double doors. Help yourself.
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing for giving prescription instead of money
jokes.punchline: Maitre d's casual direction to dumpster dive for the prescription
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan cruelty - the maitre d's matter-of-fact directions for humiliating task

jokes.text: Dumpster diving for the prescription
jokes.setup: They need to find the prescription in restaurant trash
jokes.punchline: Larry literally diving into dumpsters for his wife's medication
jokes.explanation: Physical escalation of Larry's mistake. Visual comedy of consequences.

jokes.text: This is a very pleasant activity. Can you lock me in a toilet after this?
jokes.setup: Larry digging through restaurant garbage for the prescription
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic comparison to even worse scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good Larry sarcasm in the midst of humiliation

jokes.text: She has a medical emergency. She has a tremendous rash.
jokes.setup: Sofia won't get off the phone despite the emergency
jokes.punchline: Richard's increasingly desperate attempts to get Sofia's attention
jokes.explanation: Shows Richard's powerlessness in his own relationship

jokes.text: You were like James Earl Ray today, with this guy.
jokes.setup: Richard describes Larry's racism incident
jokes.punchline: Compares Larry to Martin Luther King Jr.'s assassin
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation of the racism accusation. Perfectly delivered dark humor with historical reference.

jokes.text: Good, now we're all happy. You're happy, he's happy, I'm happy. She's sick and you're not, how's that?
jokes.setup: Richard finally getting Sofia off the phone
jokes.punchline: Sofia's sarcastic breakdown of the situation's unfairness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Sofia's resentment and self-centeredness

jokes.text: Mind your own fucking business here!
jokes.setup: Sofia interrupting the important phone call
jokes.punchline: Richard explodes at his girlfriend while trying to help Larry
jokes.explanation: Relationship tension exploding at the worst possible moment. Standard cringe escalation.

jokes.text: Navigation system celebration and father praise
jokes.setup: Larry figures out the car's navigation system
jokes.punchline: Treats this minor tech victory like a major life achievement worthy of parental pride
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neurosis - overvaluing minor accomplishments and craving parental approval.

jokes.text: "Daddy, I'm not so stupid!"
jokes.setup: Larry's excitement about his navigation success
jokes.punchline: His imagined childlike victory cry to his father
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's childlike relationship with parental approval

jokes.text: God Almighty, I can't believe I said that.
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing to Dr. Grambs for his earlier comment
jokes.punchline: His theatrical self-flagellation
jokes.explanation: Larry's typical over-the-top self-recrimination

jokes.text: And again, I'm so sorry about.... God Almighty, I can't believe I said that. I think I might have overreacted.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to apologize to Dr. Grambs
jokes.punchline: Dr. Grambs graciously accepting that he may have overreacted
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing potential resolution before it gets worse

jokes.text: Eat. You got peanuts and cookies. Drinks. You're probably having a very stimulating conversation... until we came in, so, believe me.... My advice to you, go back, because....
jokes.setup: Party guests wanting to hear what Larry said
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate attempt to distract them with party refreshments
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's desperation to avoid repeating his comment

jokes.text: I am apologizing for pretty much nothing. It is nothing. It's so unimportant.
jokes.setup: Being forced to explain his offensive comment
jokes.punchline: Larry's attempt to minimize while simultaneously apologizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry contradiction - apologizing profusely for something he claims is nothing

jokes.text: You let him work on you even with the whole affirmative action thing?
jokes.setup: Larry finally reveals his original racist comment to the party
jokes.punchline: The full quote is even worse when said to a room of black academics
jokes.explanation: Climactic revelation that's even more cringe in context. Maximum impact from delayed payoff.

jokes.text: "You let him work on you even with the whole affirmative action thing?"
jokes.setup: Forced to repeat his offensive comment to the party
jokes.punchline: The full reveal of his cringeworthy original statement
jokes.explanation: The payoff moment - finally revealing the full offensive comment to maximum cringe effect

jokes.text: It was a joke. It was a bad joke. I was trying to be too affable. I went overboard in my affability.
jokes.setup: The shocked silence after Larry's revealed comment
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued rationalization using the word 'affability'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - blaming his racism on excessive friendliness

jokes.text: I don't think white people should have anything! I think we should be sleeping on the street, eating crumbs.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to prove he's not racist
jokes.punchline: Overcompensates with extreme reverse racism fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry overcompensation - goes so far in the other direction it becomes absurd. High quotability.

jokes.text: He's much better when he has a chance to write it, and look it over.
jokes.setup: Larry's awkward attempt at damage control
jokes.punchline: Dr. Grambs' cutting reference to Larry's professional comedy writing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Larry's improvisation vs. written comedy, delivered as character assassination

jokes.text: I was about to say 'sister,' but I can't....
jokes.setup: Larry catches himself about to use potentially problematic language
jokes.punchline: Self-censors in real-time while announcing the self-censorship
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy about racial sensitivity while being racially insensitive. Brilliant self-aware cringe.

jokes.text: My friends, this is Larry David... the man who refused to hire a sister for a job... because he's a racist, a bona fide racist
jokes.setup: Denise appears at the doctor's house party
jokes.punchline: Publicly denounces Larry as a racist to the entire black academic gathering
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and convergence of storylines. Maximum social mortification for Larry.

jokes.text: You could scratch yourself to death! I feel like I could scratch myself to death... if it goes untreated.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking if it's life-threatening
jokes.punchline: The absurd interpretation of dermatitis as potentially fatal
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - stretching 'life-threatening' to justify weekend doctor call

jokes.text: You could scratch yourself to death!
jokes.setup: Doctor asks if it's life-threatening
jokes.punchline: Larry argues scratching could theoretically be fatal
jokes.explanation: Larry's desperate logic to justify the weekend call. Characteristic overstatement.

jokes.text: Are you trying to bribe me? Do I look like I can be bribed? Take your money! Nobody bribes a pharmacist!
jokes.setup: Larry tries to bribe the pharmacist with the same technique
jokes.punchline: Pharmacist is outraged by the attempted bribery
jokes.explanation: Callback showing bribery doesn't work everywhere. Good escalation ending with ejection.
